ing appli-
cations for jobs, for starting to take competitive examination, or
appearing before a committee of interviewers, or going on a
journey, one is naturally very apprehensive about the result.
When he finds himself helpless, he is forced to follow the elders
in the house regarding the choice of the time and day for the

WHAT IS PANCANGA ? 299
chosen project. One may ask: Even if one selects a good time
for writing one's application for a post, if one does not get even
an Interview Call, what value can be attached to the good
Muhurta one has chosen? There is a saying in Sanskrit which
means that if you fail in spite of your efforts, you are not to blame.
As we are born to a hoary cultural tradition, we are bound to
observe its principles. Otherwise we would be going against our
own nature. The argument why you should respect the sayings of
the great Seers of yore who had no axe to grind, is that it is better
to fail by following the advice of noble persons rather than succeed
